summary: Test suite has comprehensive unit tests covering core functions (parsing, license lookup, compliance classification) but violates the explicit requirement that 'ALL TESTS MUST RUN THROUGH ACT'—LicenseChecker.Tests.ps1 unit tests execute directly via Invoke-Pester rather than through the GitHub Actions workflow. Workflow integration tests (Workflow.Tests.ps1) cover only 2 scenarios (clean run, mixed denied/unknown) via act, missing important coverage: requirements.txt format in workflow tests, exit code validation, error scenarios (malformed config/lookup JSON), peerDependencies/optionalDependencies explicit testing, and -Quiet flag behavior. Rigor is weak on edge cases (empty allow/deny lists, unusual package names, very large manifests) and workflow-level error handling. Design is reasonable (good Pester patterns, proper cleanup, descriptive names) but the architectural violation (unit tests should not run directly per requirements) and incomplete workflow test integration are significant flaws. Would catch happy-path regressions but miss edge cases and error handling scenarios.
